word_list.word: a friend's frown is better than a fool's smile
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 f晒藞蓻ndz f晒藞a蕣n 瑟z b藞蓻删蓺 冒藢忙n蓹 f藞u藧lz sm藞a瑟l

senses.definition: used to suggest that honest feedback or reprimand, even if it may be initially unpleasant, holds more value and benefit than false or empty gestures

word_list.word: all are not friends that speak us fair
word_list.pronunciation: 藞蓴藧l 蓱藧晒 n藢蓱藧t f晒藞蓻ndz 冒忙t sp藞i藧k 藢蕦s f藞蓻晒

senses.definition: used to imply that kind words or flattery from someone does not necessarily indicate true friendship

examples.x: My boss seemed to appreciate my work and constantly praised me, but I soon realized that she had no intention of promoting or giving me a raise, reminding me that all are not friends that speak us fair.

word_list.word: {not} fear the enemy that attacks you, but the fake friend that hugs you
word_list.pronunciation: n藢蓱藧t f藞瑟晒 冒瑟 藞蓻n蓹mi 冒忙t 蓯t藞忙ks ju藧 b藢蕦t 冒蓹 f藞e瑟k f晒藞蓻nd 冒忙t h藞蕦伞z ju藧

senses.definition: used to warn against the potential harm caused by those who pretend to be friends but have ulterior motives, as they can deceive and betray a person from within

word_list.word: false friends are (much|far|way|) worse than open enemies
word_list.pronunciation: f藞蓱藧ls f晒藞蓻ndz 蓱藧晒 m藞蕦t蕛 蓴藧晒 f藞蓱藧晒 蓴藧晒 w藞e瑟 w藞蓽藧s 冒蓯n 藞o蕣p蓹n 藞蓻n蓹m瑟z

senses.definition: used to imply that it is more dangerous to have friends who pretend to be loyal while secretly betraying one than to have enemies who are open about their hostility toward one

examples.x: When she found out that her best friend had been spreading malicious rumors about her, she realized that false friends are worse than open enemies.

word_list.pronunciation: f藞a瑟n w藞蓽藧dz 蕛藢忙l 蓴藧晒 w瑟l 蓴藧晒 b藞蕦删蓺 n藞o蕣 p藞蓱藧晒sn瑟ps

senses.definition: used to emphasize that empty words or flattery are not sufficient to accomplish a task or reach a goal; rather, it is the action and effort that count

examples.x: The politician's speeches were filled with beautiful words and promises, but unfortunately, fine words butter no parsnips, and he failed to deliver on most of his campaign pledges.

word_list.word: flattery, (much|) like perfume, (should|must|shall) be smelled (but|and) not swallowed
word_list.pronunciation: fl藞忙删蓺晒i m藞蕦t蕛 蓴藧晒 l藞a瑟k p藞蓽藧fju藧m 蕛藢蕣d 蓴藧晒 m藞蕦st 蓴藧晒 蕛藢忙l bi藧 sm藞蓻ld b藢蕦t 蓴藧晒 忙nd n藢蓱藧t sw藞蓱藧lo蕣d

senses.definition: used to advise that while flattering words may be pleasant to hear, one should not take them too seriously or believe them blindly

word_list.word: imitation is the sincerest form of flattery
word_list.pronunciation: 藢瑟m瑟t藞e瑟蕛蓹n 瑟z 冒蓹 s瑟ns藞i蓹晒蓹st f藞蓴藧晒m 蕦v fl藞忙删蓺晒i

senses.definition: used to suggest that copying or imitating someone else's behavior or style is a way of paying them a compliment or showing admiration for them

examples.x: If someone copies your fashion style, it can be a way of showing that they appreciate your taste - imitation is the sincerest form of flattery.
